headline: Berkshire Bought Macy's And Delta Air Lines After Buffett's Exit
summary: Warren Buffett's successor Greg Abel is steering both day-to-day operations and a massive investment portfolio.

headline: Berkshire adds Delta and Macy’s stakes in first quarter
summary: Investing.com -- Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE:BRKa) disclosed new positions in Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL) and Macy’s (NYSE:M) during the first quarter under Chief Executive Greg Abel, according to a regulatory filing Friday afternoon. Shares rallied 3% and 5%, respectively, after hours on Friday amid the news.

Shares rallied 3% and 5%, respectively, after hours on Friday amid the news.

headline: Delta Air Lines Tests Event Driven Trans Atlantic Capacity With Football Flights
summary: Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL) is adding special trans Atlantic flights tied to major football events. The company is increasing capacity on select routes to handle event driven international travel demand. These flights are positioned as temporary additions rather than routine schedule adjustments. For investors watching NYSE:DAL, this move sits alongside a share price of $71.55 and a 1 year return of 44.0%. Over 3 years the stock has returned 106.0%, and over 5 years 62.8%, which gives useful...

headline: Buffett Steps Aside: Abel's First Berkshire 13F Buys Delta Air Lines, Exits Visa And Mastercard
summary: Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE:BRK-A)(NYSE:BRK-B) is no longer run by Warren Buffett, with Greg Abel as CEO. New positions in Delta, Macy's, Alphabet C. Exits include Visa, Mastercard, UnitedHealth, Domino's, Aon, Pool, Amazon, Heico, Formula One.
